name: A9-DPC transplantation showed early Parkinson graft-survival and motor signals
summary: A phase 1/2a Parkinson disease trial reported no graft-related adverse events, PET uptake consistent with graft survival, and exploratory motor improvement after hESC-derived A9 dopamine progenitor transplantation.
statement: In 12 people with moderate-to-severe Parkinson disease, hESC-derived A9 dopamine progenitor transplantation had no dose-limiting toxicities or graft-related adverse events and showed 12-month exploratory motor and dopamine-transporter PET signals.
population_or_model: People with moderate-to-severe Parkinson disease in a single-center open-label dose-escalation trial
quantitative_note: The report states that off-medication MDS-UPDRS Part III scores and Hoehn and Yahr stage improved, with greater motor improvement and posterior putamen dopamine-transporter PET uptake in the high-dose group.
caveats: The study was open-label, single-center, and had only 12 participants.
Exploratory motor changes require controlled confirmation.
The evidence is disease-specific neuronal replacement, not broad aging or LEV-relevant efficacy.
